title: adenium obesum subsp. somalense
intro: Adenium obesum subsp. somalense, commonly known as the Somali Desert Rose, is a succulent plant species native to East Africa. It possesses medicinal properties, primarily attributed to its roots and leaves, which contain alkaloids and flavonoids that have been used to treat various health conditions. The Somali Desert Rose has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, including infusions and decoctions, are used in folk medicine, but caution is advised due to potential toxicity and limited scientific research.
